pattern: Each row is built from the previous one: interior entries are the sum of the two values diagonally above, with ones on both ends.
hints: Every row begins and ends with 1, so only the interior numbers need real work.
An interior entry at position j of a row equals the sum of positions j-1 and j of the row right above it.
Start with [[1]]. For each new row, walk the previous row and add adjacent pairs to fill the middle, padding 1 at the front and back.

edge_inputs.description: three rows introduces the first interior sum

stress.description: a sweep of row counts up to the constraint maximum
stress.note: values stay exact integers (no overflow concern in python/js for n<=30)
